what_it_does: Implements forkVBrain.attach, which builds a brain UI inside a host panel for the V/RIGHT-HAND-MAN overlay, reads the current aim address live at send time, and routes operator input to either a streaming Claude Code turn or a blocking grounded-mind answer. It also provides helpers to post to a channel by human name and to write direction annotations back at the current aim.
observations: Mounts a DOM subtree with .v-brain-* classes inside the supplied panelEl idempotently.
Reads the aim address live at send time so re-aiming between turns is automatic.
Uses getAimLabel to show a human label in .v-brain-aim and never displays the raw address.
Exports two ask paths: ask() streams via forkBrainCore.talk to /api/claude/turn, and groundedAsk() blocks via /api/brain/ask.
The default send button and Enter key invoke groundedAsk, not the streaming ask().
groundedAsk self-renders a thinking placeholder and then the stripped answer into .v-brain-reply.
postToChannel translates backend error text into human-readable failure messages.
direct() routes a direction/reaction/favour annotation at the current aim through core.writeDirections.
Assumes window.forkBrainCore is already loaded and throws if it is missing.
standouts: Dual brain paths co-located in one module with a single render slot.
Operator-law enforcement: raw addresses and backend errors are hidden from the user.
The default V ask is the blocking grounded mind rather than the streaming Claude Code path.
conventions: Immediately-invoked function expression attaching a namespace to window.
Fail-soft fetch handlers that render a fallback note and return an error-shaped object.
Relies on an external global dependency (window.forkBrainCore) rather than importing it.
concerns: destroy() removes the DOM subtree but does not explicitly detach the event listeners registered on sendEl and inputEl.
ask() returns null on failure while groundedAsk() returns an error object, so error shapes are inconsistent.
The human error mapping in _humanPostError is keyword-based and could misclassify new backend messages.
There is no input sanitization or length limiting before sending to the APIs.
notes: This is the V panel's brain glue: it builds the operator-facing ask UI, routes questions to the current aim, and keeps raw addresses and backend errors out of sight.
questions: What exact shape does forkBrainCore.talk return and does it also write into replyEl?
How does the host update getAimAddress/getAimLabel and is aimChanged() called automatically on external aim changes?
What does core.stripMd remove and does it handle all markdown the backend might return?
What channel names are valid and how does the backend resolve a human name to a channel?
What fields are required in the item passed to direct()?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file builds a per-page, aim-aware brain UI with the documented ask, grounded-ask, channel-post, and direction paths.
apparent_themes: Operator-law: hide implementation details and machine names from the user.
Single-source rendering: the module owns both brain paths and the reply slot.
Aim-as-context: every turn is grounded to a live address read at send time.
Fail-soft UX: never leave a blank middle; always render a note.
intention_signals: Make the bottom-right V host a persistent, page-local conversational assistant that can answer about whatever it is currently aimed at, post openly to named channels, and route feedback back to the addressed element, all while speaking in human terms rather than system addresses.
novelty: The dual-path design that keeps a streaming Claude Code turn and a fast blocking grounded-mind answer in the same UI, with the slower grounded path as the default send behavior.
contribution: A self-contained V-panel brain attachment that wires operator input to aim-aware AI turns, channel posts, and directional feedback, enforcing address hiding and consistent in-panel rendering.
summary_for_embedding: fork-v-brain.js is the V overlay's brain attachment module that builds an ask UI inside a host panel, routes user prompts to either a streaming Claude Code turn or a blocking grounded-mind answer at the current aim address, posts to named channels, and writes direction annotations back to the aimed element.
intention_for_embedding: To give the V/RIGHT-HAND-MAN overlay a persistent, per-page conversational brain that talks about the currently aimed target and can act on the operator's behalf.
suggested_fields: aim_address_resolution: how the host maps UI targets to address strings
brain_path_latency: expected response times for streaming vs grounded paths
channel_name_registry: mapping of human channel names to backend channels
error_translation_table: canonical mapping from backend errors to human messages
proposed_edge_kinds: owns_render_slot: module is the single renderer for a DOM region
enforces_operator_law: module translates or hides machine identifiers for users
default_path: one of several exported functions is the primary user trigger

symbols.does: Builds the V brain UI, binds events, and returns the public API.
symbols.description: Validates the required panel element and aim getter, ensures a .v-brain subtree exists inside panelEl, wires the send button and Enter key to groundedAsk, and exposes ask, groundedAsk, postToChannel, direct, aimChanged, and destroy.

symbols.does: Returns the human-readable aim label or a generic fallback.
symbols.description: Safely calls the optional getAimLabel callback and falls back to 'this', isolating label resolution from the raw address so the UI never shows machine identifiers.

symbols.does: Updates the .v-brain-aim header with the current human label.
symbols.description: Renders 'Ask about: <label>' so the operator sees meaning rather than the raw aim address; called during attach and exposed for the host to invoke on re-aim.

symbols.does: Sends a streaming Claude Code turn about the current aim.
symbols.description: Reads the live aim address, clears the input, and delegates rendering and conversation continuity to forkBrainCore.talk for the /api/claude/turn path.

symbols.does: Sends a blocking grounded-mind question and self-renders the answer.
symbols.description: POSTs to /api/brain/ask with an optional aim, shows a thinking placeholder in .v-brain-reply, then renders the stripped answer or a clear failure note and returns the parsed response.

symbols.does: Maps backend post errors to human-readable phrases.
symbols.description: Keyword-matches error strings for archived, missing, member, and empty conditions and returns a short plain-language reason, keeping raw backend messages out of the UI.

symbols.does: Posts a message to a channel by human name and shows a transparency line.
symbols.description: POSTs {channel, message} to /api/channel/post, renders either a confirmation that the V posted or a human-translated error, and returns the server response.

symbols.does: Writes a direction annotation back at the current aim.
symbols.description: Reads the live aim address and calls forkBrainCore.writeDirections with an element_id and the supplied direction item.

symbols.does: Removes the .v-brain DOM subtree from the panel.
symbols.description: Detaches the built UI from panelEl; it does not explicitly remove the event listeners registered on sendEl and inputEl.
